project: PhaseKit: Mean Field Phase Diagrams for Neural Network Initialization
triage_summary: Three-class phase prediction at 56% barely above chance; ~100% per-layer variance error; missing formal bounds on O(1/N^3) terms

tasks.description: train_network_numpy uses ReLU derivative (acts[l] > 0) for ALL activations including tanh/GELU/SiLU. This produces incorrect empirical ground truth for non-ReLU activations, directly causing the poor 3-class accuracy.
tasks.resolution: Added activation_derivative_np() with per-activation derivatives (tanh, gelu, silu). v4 experiments use correct derivatives.

tasks.description: determine_empirical_phase labels everything with 0.3 < loss_ratio < 0.85 as 'critical', which is far too broad. Ordered-phase networks that train moderately get mislabeled as critical.
The soft phase classifier's epsilon is too narrow, especially for GELU/SiLU where the ordered phase spans a wide sigma_w range. Need activation-specific width scaling.
tasks.resolution: Activation-specific constants c_relu=1.0, c_tanh=1.2, c_gelu=c_silu=1.8 implemented.

tasks.description: Cross-layer cumulative_correction grows unboundedly and averaging by 1/(l+1) is ad hoc. Replace with proper perturbative propagation where each layer's correction decays rather than accumulates.
Add Theorem on formal bounds for O(1/N^3) truncation error in variance recursion. The remainder is bounded by C * sigma_w^8 * M_8(q) / N^3 where M_8 is the 8th moment.
Replace ambiguous L*|chi_1-1|*D/N << 1 with concrete validated threshold: validity when correction_ratio = |delta_q / q_mf| < 0.3, validated empirically.
Add numerical quadrature error analysis for kappa_4 computation via scipy.integrate.quad. Show relative error < 1e-6 across all activations.

tasks.task: Update grounding.json with new data sources
